evals.prompt: I just watched this talk by Rich Hickey on Simple Made Easy — can you summarize the key ideas and make a study guide? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SxdOUGdseq4
evals.expected_output: Creates a notebook, adds the YouTube video as a source, asks analytical questions, generates a study guide report, and downloads it to an organized directory structure.

assertions.name: creates-notebook
assertions.description: Creates a dedicated notebook for this research task

assertions.name: adds-youtube-source
assertions.description: Adds the YouTube URL as a source using 'source add'

assertions.name: waits-for-processing
assertions.description: Waits for source processing before chat or generation

assertions.name: generates-study-guide
assertions.description: Generates a report with --format study-guide (not just chat)

assertions.name: organizes-output
assertions.description: Downloads output to a structured directory (~/Documents/notebooklm/... or similar)

assertions.name: analytical-questions
assertions.description: Asks targeted analytical questions before generating the study guide

evals.prompt: I need to understand the current landscape of WebAssembly runtimes — what's out there, how they compare, what's mature vs experimental. Give me a comprehensive overview.
evals.expected_output: Creates a research notebook, runs deep web research with targeted queries, waits for sources, asks cross-referencing analytical questions, generates a briefing doc and mind map, downloads outputs to organized directories.

assertions.name: uses-deep-research
assertions.description: Uses --mode deep for comprehensive source gathering

assertions.name: multiple-queries
assertions.description: Runs more than one research query to cover different angles

assertions.name: waits-for-sources
assertions.description: Waits for all sources to be ready before analysis

assertions.name: cross-referencing-questions
assertions.description: Asks analytical questions that compare/contrast across sources

assertions.name: multiple-output-types
assertions.description: Generates more than one output type (e.g., report + mind map)

evals.prompt: Help me create a quick-start tutorial for someone learning Deno. They know Node.js already. I want something I can share with my team.
evals.expected_output: Creates a notebook, adds official Deno docs and tutorials as sources, generates a custom report with tutorial-focused instructions targeting Node.js developers, downloads to an organized directory.

assertions.name: curated-sources
assertions.description: Adds specific relevant sources (official docs, tutorials) rather than only research

assertions.name: custom-report-instructions
assertions.description: Uses --format custom or --append to tailor report for the audience

assertions.name: audience-targeting
assertions.description: Report instructions mention target audience (Node.js developers)

assertions.name: shareable-output
assertions.description: Produces a downloadable artifact the user can share (not just chat)
